Leaderboard  ›  dr_cross_deep_0019

Task dr_cross_deep_0019

Comparison Travel tier: deep difficulty: 5/5 shopping + reddit + wikipedia 10 scored
Task prompt
The exact text the agent receives. No hints, no tools beyond the sandbox + LLM.
Produce a Comparison report on visa-free destinations for 4 passport tiers — (P1) US, (P2) UK, (P3) Japan, (P4) Singapore — across 5 destination categories: (D1) Schengen Europe, (D2) East Asia, (D3) Southeast Asia, (D4) Latin America, (D5) Africa. Each cell = number of visa-free destinations + max stay duration + any visa-on-arrival exceptions. Ground in >= 120 sandbox URLs, cite >= 60. (A) `__SHOPPING__` >= 10 travel guides / passport holders / luggage + price + rating. (B) `__REDDIT__` >= 30 threads from /f/travel, /f/digitalnomad, /f/onebag, /f/IWantOut, /f/expats, /f/JapanTravel, /f/solotravel. (C) `__WIKIPEDIA__` >= 25 articles, mandatory: Visa policy of the United States, Visa policy of Japan, Visa policy of Singapore, Visa policy of the United Kingdom, Henley Passport Index, Schengen Area, Visa on arrival, Electronic travel authorization, Travel visa, Visa-free entry. Output a 4 (passport) x 5 (region) matrix + 'best passport for digital nomads' analysis. Format: markdown links only. Begin with matrix.
Hard constraints
Markdown spec
  • ≥ 3500 words
  • ≤ 8000 words
  • ≥ 25 paragraphs
  • ≥ 60 citations
  • ≥ 120 distinct URLs cited
Citation policy
  • ≥ 60 distinct sources
  • ≥ 3 distinct domains
  • __SHOPPING__: ≥ 10
  • __REDDIT__: ≥ 30
  • __WIKIPEDIA__: ≥ 25
Synthesis requirements
  • task_type: comparison
  • comparison_paths: ['us', 'uk', 'jp', 'sg']
  • use_cases: ['schengen', 'east_asia', 'se_asia', 'latin_america', 'africa']
  • matrix_cells_required: 20
  • min_evidence_per_cell: {'shopping': 1, 'reddit': 1, 'wikipedia': 1}
Agent ranking on this task
composite_v2 · multiplicative reachability gate
